analyzeComplexity.name: analyzeComplexity
analyzeComplexity.description: Analyzes code complexity metrics for files or projects

parameters.name: file_path
parameters.description: Path to the file or directory to analyze

parameters.name: recursive
parameters.description: Whether to recursively analyze directories: -r/--recursive for unlimited, -rN/--recursiveN for N levels

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Output format: "detailed" or "summary"

examples.description: Analyze complexity of a single Python file
Recursively analyze all code files in a directory
Generate a summary report analyzing up to 2 levels deep

analyzeComplexity.class_doc:
Command to analyze code complexity metrics for development environments

This version uses the centralized recursive file finder utility.
analyzeComplexity.execute_doc:
Analyzes code complexity of files or directories

Args:
    file_path: Path to file or directory to analyze
    recursive: Whether to recursively analyze directories: -r/--recursive for unlimited, -rN/--recursiveN for N levels
    format: Output format (detailed or summary)
    
Returns:
    Analysis results as string

compareFiles.name: compareFiles
compareFiles.description: Compara dos archivos y muestra sus diferencias, útil para IDE

parameters.name: file1
parameters.description: Ruta al primer archivo

parameters.name: file2
parameters.description: Ruta al segundo archivo

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: Modo de comparación: "full" (completo), "summary" (resumen) o "percent" (porcentaje)

examples.description: Comparar dos archivos Python y mostrar todas las diferencias
Mostrar un resumen de diferencias entre dos archivos de texto
Mostrar el porcentaje de similitud entre dos archivos JavaScript

compareFiles.class_doc:
Comando para comparar dos archivos y mostrar sus diferencias

This version uses the centralized recursive parameter utility.
compareFiles.execute_doc:
Compara dos archivos y muestra sus diferencias

Args:
    file1: Ruta al primer archivo
    file2: Ruta al segundo archivo
    mode: Modo de comparación (full, summary, percent)
    
Returns:
    Resultado de la comparación en el formato especificado

createDocTemplatePython.name: createDocTemplatePython
createDocTemplatePython.description: Creates basic documentation templates for Python code that lacks docstrings

parameters.name: file_path
parameters.description: Path to the Python file to process

parameters.name: style
parameters.description: Documentation style (google, numpy, sphinx)

parameters.name: overwrite
parameters.description: Whether to overwrite existing docstrings

parameters.name: preview
parameters.description: Preview changes without modifying the file

examples.description: Create Google-style documentation templates for myfile.py
Create Sphinx-style documentation templates for myfile.py

examples.command: qzx CreateDocTemplatePython myfile.py google true false
examples.description: Create Google-style documentation templates, overwriting existing ones

examples.command: qzx CreateDocTemplatePython myfile.py google false true
examples.description: Preview Google-style documentation templates without modifying the file

createDocTemplatePython.class_doc: Command to generate documentation templates for Python code
createDocTemplatePython.execute_doc:
Generates documentation templates for Python code

Args:
    file_path (str): Path to the Python file to process
    style (str, optional): Documentation style (google, numpy, sphinx)
    overwrite (bool, optional): Whether to overwrite existing docstrings
    preview (bool, optional): Preview changes without modifying the file
    
Returns:
    Dictionary with the result of the operation

findDeadCode.name: findDeadCode
findDeadCode.description: Scans files for declared functions and classes, and identifies those with zero references in other files

parameters.name: scan_path
parameters.description: Path to scan for files (defaults to current directory)

examples.description: Scan the current directory for dead code
Scan the src/ directory

findDeadCode.class_doc: Command to identify dead code (unused classes, functions, or exports) in a codebase.
findDeadCode.execute_doc:
Executes dead code detection

Args:
    scan_path (str): Path to scan
    
Returns:
    Dictionary with dead code analysis details

traceCircularImports.name: traceCircularImports
traceCircularImports.description: Traces module imports recursively and identifies circular import dependencies (loops)

parameters.name: scan_path
parameters.description: Path to scan for Python files (defaults to current directory)

examples.description: Search for circular imports in the current directory
Search for circular imports specifically inside src/qzx

traceCircularImports.class_doc: Command to detect circular import dependencies in Python projects.
traceCircularImports.execute_doc:
Executes circular imports detection

Args:
    scan_path (str): Path to scan
    
Returns:
    Dictionary with circular import analysis details

traceEnvVar.name: traceEnvVar
traceEnvVar.description: Traces usage of an environment variable across code files, .env, and .env.example templates

parameters.name: var_name
parameters.description: Name of the environment variable to search for (e.g. DATABASE_URL, PORT)

parameters.name: project_path
parameters.description: Path to the project root directory (defaults to current directory)

parameters.name: recursive
parameters.description: Whether to search directories recursively (defaults to True)

examples.description: Trace usage of the PORT environment variable in the current directory
Trace usage of STRIPE_API_KEY in C:/my-project

traceEnvVar.class_doc: Command to trace environment variable usage across files, templates, and codebases.
traceEnvVar.execute_doc:
Executes the trace environment variable diagnostic

Args:
    var_name (str): Name of environment variable
    project_path (str): Path to search
    recursive (bool/str): Recursive search setting
    
Returns:
    Dictionary with trace diagnostic details

auditLanguages.name: auditLanguages
auditLanguages.description: Audits a project workspace for programming languages and alerts on underrepresented ones in QZX

parameters.name: scan_path
parameters.description: Path to scan for programming language files (defaults to current directory)

parameters.name: threshold
parameters.description: Minimum file count threshold to analyze a language (default is 3)

examples.description: Audit languages in the current directory
Audit languages in the src/ directory with a minimum file count of 5

auditLanguages.class_doc: Command to audit underrepresented programming languages in a project and trigger alerts.
auditLanguages.execute_doc:
Executes the language representation audit

Args:
    scan_path (str): Path to scan
    threshold (int/str): Minimum file count to trigger audits
    
Returns:
    Dictionary containing audit details and warnings

cleanDevCaches.name: cleanDevCaches
cleanDevCaches.description: Scans recursively for heavy development caches/dependencies (node_modules, __pycache__, dist) and purges them

parameters.name: scan_path
parameters.description: Path to start the scan from (defaults to current working directory)

parameters.name: dry_run
parameters.description: If true, only lists files and space saved without deleting anything (true/false)

parameters.name: max_depth
parameters.description: Maximum folder depth to traverse recursively (defaults to 4)

examples.description: Scan the current directory for cache folders (dry run)
Scan the current directory and actually delete found cache folders

cleanDevCaches.class_doc: Command to identify and clean up heavy development cache/dependency directories (e.g. node_modules, __pycache__).
cleanDevCaches.execute_doc:
Executes the cache scanning and cleaning

Args:
    scan_path (str): The starting directory path
    dry_run (str/bool): Whether to skip actual deletion
    max_depth (str/int): Traversal depth limit
    
Returns:
    Dictionary with results and details

parameters.description: Path to the directory to scan (defaults to the current working directory)

getGitStatus.execute_doc:

Returns:

getProjectTree.name: getProjectTree
getProjectTree.description: Generates a clean visual directory tree (ASCII and JSON) excluding heavy directories like node_modules

parameters.name: dir_path
parameters.description: Path to the directory to visualize (defaults to current directory)

parameters.name: max_depth
parameters.description: Maximum depth level of the tree (defaults to 2)

parameters.name: exclude_dirs
parameters.description: Comma-separated directory names to exclude (defaults to standard caches and configs)

parameters.name: include_files
parameters.description: Whether to list files as well as directories (true/false)

examples.description: Show directory tree for the current folder up to 2 levels deep
Show directory tree for the src folder up to 3 levels deep
Show directory tree excluding only .git and node_modules, showing folders only

getProjectTree.class_doc: Command to visualize a directory structure as a clean text-based tree or JSON model, excluding heavy or private files.
getProjectTree.execute_doc:
Executes directory tree generation

Args:
    dir_path (str): The starting directory path
    max_depth (str/int): Maximum depth to traverse
    exclude_dirs (str, optional): Commas separated exclude folder list
    include_files (str/bool): Whether to include files in the tree
    
Returns:
    Dictionary containing ASCII tree text and JSON tree model

parameters.name: with_tests
parameters.description: Whether to include testing scaffolding

parameters.name: build_system
parameters.description: Build system to use (make, cmake, or none)

changePermissions.name: changePermissions

parameters.description: Path to the file or directory

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: Permission mode in octal (e.g., 755) or string format (e.g., "a+x")

parameters.name: recursive
parameters.description: Whether to apply permissions recursively: -r/--recursive for unlimited, -rN/--recursiveN for N levels

Change directory permissions up to 2 levels deep

changePermissions.class_doc:
Command to change file or directory permissions (similar to chmod)

This version uses the centralized recursive file finder utility.
changePermissions.execute_doc:
Changes permissions of a file or directory

Args:
    path (str): Path to the file or directory
    mode (str/int): Permission mode in octal (e.g., 755) or string format (e.g., "a+x")
    recursive: Recursion level: none by default, -r/--recursive for unlimited, -rN/--recursiveN for N levels
    
Returns:
    Operation result

Compress the src folder into src.zip

Compresses a file or directory

    zip_path (str): Destination ZIP file path
    source_path (str): Source directory or file
    exclude_patterns (str, optional): Exclude rules comma-separated
    
Returns:
    Dictionary with compression results

copyFile.name: copyFile
copyFile.description: Copies a file or directory from source to destination

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: Path to the source file or directory

parameters.name: destination
parameters.description: Path to the destination file or directory

parameters.name: recursive
parameters.description: For directories: -r/--recursive to copy all contents, -rN/--recursiveN for N levels deep

parameters.name: force
parameters.description: Whether to overwrite the destination if it exists

examples.description: Copy a file to another file
Copy a file to a directory
Copy a directory recursively (all contents)
Copy a directory recursively up to 2 levels deep
Copy a directory recursively and overwrite if exists

copyFile.class_doc:
Command to copy a file or directory

Supports flags:
-r, -R, --recursive: Enable unlimited recursive directory copy
-rN, --recursiveN: Enable recursive directory copy up to N levels deep

This version uses the centralized recursive parameter utility.
copyFile.execute_doc:
Copies a file or directory

Args:
    source (str): Path to the source file or directory
    destination (str): Path to the destination file or directory
    recursive: Recursion level: none by default, -r/--recursive for unlimited, -rN/--recursiveN for N levels
    force (bool, optional): Whether to overwrite the destination if it exists
    
Returns:
    Success message or error

countLinesInFile.name: countLinesInFile

parameters.name: file_path
parameters.description: Path to the file(s) to count lines in. Supports wildcards like "*.py"

parameters.name: recursive
parameters.description: Recursion level: -r/--recursive for unlimited depth, -rN/--recursiveN for N levels deep

parameters.name: ignore_empty
parameters.description: Whether to ignore empty lines when counting

examples.description: Count the number of lines in a Python script
Count the number of lines in a log file
Count the number of lines in all Python files in the current directory
Count the number of lines in all JavaScript files in the src directory and its subdirectories
Count the number of non-empty lines in all text files in the current directory and subdirectories up to 2 levels deep

countLinesInFile.class_doc:
Command to count the number of lines in files with support for wildcards and recursive searching

Supports flags:
-r, -R, --recursive: Enable unlimited recursive directory search
-rN, --recursiveN: Enable recursive directory search up to N levels deep

This version uses the centralized recursive file finder utility.
countLinesInFile.execute_doc:
Counts the number of lines in files with support for wildcards and recursive searching

Args:
    file_path: Path to the file(s) to count lines in. Supports wildcards like "*.py"
    recursive: Recursion level: none by default, -r/--recursive for unlimited, -rN/--recursiveN for N levels
    ignore_empty: Whether to ignore empty lines when counting
    
Returns:
    Dictionary with line count results or error message

Args:
    scan_path (str): Root folder to scan
    min_size_kb (str/int): Size threshold to filter files
    max_depth (str/int): Folder depth limit
    
Returns:
    Dictionary containing duplicate file lists and disk reclaim details

findFiles.name: findFiles
findFiles.description: Advanced file search with multiple filtering options (like find + grep combined)

parameters.name: search_path
parameters.description: Path to start the search from

parameters.name: pattern
parameters.description: File name pattern to search for (supports glob syntax: *.txt, data-???.csv)

parameters.name: recursive
parameters.description: Recursion level: -r/--recursive for unlimited depth, -rN/--recursiveN for N levels deep

parameters.name: max_depth
parameters.description: Maximum directory depth to search (null for unlimited)

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Filter by type: "f" (files), "d" (directories), or "l" (links)

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: Filter by size (e.g., +1M for >1MB, -500K for <500KB, 10K for exactly 10KB)

parameters.name: min_size
parameters.description: Minimum file size in bytes or with KB/MB/GB suffix

parameters.name: max_size
parameters.description: Maximum file size in bytes or with KB/MB/GB suffix

parameters.name: mtime
parameters.description: Filter by modification time (e.g., +7 for >7 days old, -2 for <2 days old)

parameters.name: newer_than
parameters.description: Files newer than specified date (YYYY-MM-DD) or "today", "yesterday"

parameters.name: older_than
parameters.description: Files older than specified date (YYYY-MM-DD) or "today", "yesterday"

parameters.name: contains
parameters.description: Only include files containing this text

parameters.name: contains_regex
parameters.description: Only include files matching this regex pattern

parameters.name: case_sensitive
parameters.description: Make content searches case sensitive (true/false)

parameters.name: exclude
parameters.description: Exclude file/directory patterns (comma-separated: *.bak,*.tmp)

parameters.name: exclude_dir
parameters.description: Exclude directory patterns (comma-separated: .git,.vscode)

parameters.name: follow_symlinks
parameters.description: Follow symbolic links (true/false)

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: Sort by: name, path, size, mtime (modification time), or none

parameters.name: reverse_sort
parameters.description: Reverse the sorting order (true/false)

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Output format: "full" (default), "name" (filename only), "detailed" (full details), "json", "csv"

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return

examples.description: Find all Python files in current directory and subdirectories
Find all JavaScript files in /src without searching subdirectories
Find all files up to 2 levels deep
Find all text files larger than 1MB

examples.command: qzx findFiles . * null null null null null null -7
examples.description: Find files modified within the last 7 days

examples.command: qzx findFiles . *.log null null null null null today null
examples.description: Find log files created or modified today

examples.command: qzx findFiles . *.py null null null null null null null TODO
examples.description: Find Python files containing the word "TODO"

examples.command: qzx findFiles . * null null null null null null null null "def\s+\w+"
examples.description: Find files containing function definitions using regex

examples.command: qzx findFiles . * null null null null null null null null null false "*.tmp,*.bak"
examples.description: Find all files excluding temp and backup files

examples.command: qzx findFiles . * true null null null null null null null null null null .git,node_modules
examples.description: Find all files excluding .git and node_modules directories

findFiles.class_doc:
Command for advanced file searching with multiple filtering options

Supports flags:
-r, -R, --recursive: Enable unlimited recursive directory search
-rN, --recursiveN: Enable recursive directory search up to N levels deep

This version uses the centralized recursive file finder utility.
findFiles.execute_doc:
Advanced file search with multiple filtering options

Args:
    search_path (str): Path to start the search from
    pattern (str): File name pattern to search for (supports glob syntax)
    recursive: Recursion level: -r/--recursive for unlimited depth, -rN/--recursiveN for N levels deep
    max_depth (int): Maximum directory depth to search (None for unlimited)
    type (str): Filter by type: "f" (files), "d" (directories), or "l" (links)
    size (str): Filter by size (e.g., +1M for >1MB, -500K for <500KB)
    min_size (str): Minimum file size in bytes or with KB/MB/GB suffix
    max_size (str): Maximum file size in bytes or with KB/MB/GB suffix
    mtime (str): Filter by modification time (e.g., +7 for >7 days old)
    newer_than (str): Files newer than specified date (YYYY-MM-DD)
    older_than (str): Files older than specified date (YYYY-MM-DD)
    contains (str): Only include files containing this text
    contains_regex (str): Only include files matching this regex pattern
    case_sensitive (bool): Make content searches case sensitive
    exclude (str): Exclude file/directory patterns (comma-separated)
    exclude_dir (str): Exclude directory patterns (comma-separated)
    follow_symlinks (bool): Follow symbolic links
    sort_by (str): Sort by: name, path, size, mtime, or none
    reverse_sort (bool): Reverse the sorting order
    format (str): Output format: "full", "name", "detailed", "json", "csv"
    limit (int): Maximum number of results to return
    
Returns:
    Search results based on specified format

findLargeFiles.name: findLargeFiles
findLargeFiles.description: Searches for files with specific extensions that exceed a given size

parameters.name: extension

parameters.description: Minimum file size in bytes

parameters.name: recursive
parameters.description: Recursion level: -r/--recursive for unlimited depth, -rN/--recursiveN for N levels deep

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: Sort results by: "name", "size", "date"

Find all .py files in src directory and subdirectories larger than 1KB
Find all .js files (non-recursive) larger than 5KB, sorted by name

findLargeFiles.class_doc:
Command to find files of specified type exceeding a minimum size

Supports flags:
-r, -R, --recursive: Enable unlimited recursive directory search
-rN, --recursiveN: Enable recursive directory search up to N levels deep

This version uses the centralized recursive file finder utility.
findLargeFiles.execute_doc:
Searches for files matching a pattern that exceed a minimum size

Args:
    directory (str): Directory to search in
    extension (str): File extension to filter by (e.g., 'jpg' or '.jpg')
    min_size (str): Minimum file size (e.g., '1MB', '500KB')
    recursive: Recursion level: none by default, -r/--recursive for unlimited, -rN/--recursiveN for N levels
    sort_by (str): Sort criteria ('size', 'name', or 'date')
    
Returns:
    Dictionary with search results

findText.name: findText
findText.description: Searches for text patterns in files with advanced filtering options

parameters.name: pattern
parameters.description: Text pattern to search for (supports regular expressions)

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: File or directory (or space-separated list of files/directories) to search in

parameters.name: recursive
parameters.description: Recursion level: -r/--recursive for unlimited depth, -rN/--recursiveN for N levels deep

parameters.name: regex
parameters.description: Use regular expressions for pattern matching (true/false)

parameters.name: case_sensitive
parameters.description: Perform case-sensitive search (true/false)

parameters.name: file_pattern
parameters.description: Only search in files matching this pattern (e.g., "*.py" or "*.{py,js}")

parameters.name: context_lines
parameters.description: Number of lines to show before and after each match

parameters.name: invert_match
parameters.description: Show lines that do NOT match the pattern (true/false)

parameters.name: count_only
parameters.description: Only show count of matches per file (true/false)

parameters.name: max_matches
parameters.description: Maximum number of matches to display (0 for unlimited)

parameters.name: colored
parameters.description: Highlight matches with color (true/false)

examples.description: Find all occurrences of "function" in a JavaScript file
Find all occurrences of "error" in all files under logs directory
Find all function definitions in Python files using regex
Find warnings or errors in log files
Find TODOs in source files with 2 lines of context
Find all occurrences of "function" in multiple specific files

findText.class_doc:
Command to search for text patterns in files with advanced options

Supports flags:
-r, -R, --recursive: Enable unlimited recursive directory search
-rN, --recursiveN: Enable recursive directory search up to N levels deep

This version uses the centralized recursive file finder utility.
findText.execute_doc:
Searches for text patterns in files with advanced options

Args:
    pattern (str): Text pattern to search for
    target (str): File or directory to search in (space-separated for multiple)
    recursive: Recursion level: none by default, -r/--recursive for unlimited, -rN/--recursiveN for N levels
    regex (bool): Whether to use regular expressions for pattern matching
    case_sensitive (bool): Whether the search should be case-sensitive
    file_pattern (str): Only search in files matching this pattern (e.g., "*.py")
    context_lines (int): Number of lines to show before and after each match
    invert_match (bool): Show lines that do NOT match the pattern
    count_only (bool): Only show count of matches per file
    max_matches (int): Maximum number of matches to display (0 for unlimited)
    colored (bool): Whether to colorize the output
    
Returns:
    Dictionary with search results

getHumanLanguageStats.name: getHumanLanguageStats
getHumanLanguageStats.description: Analyzes files to determine the percentage of text in different languages. Improved version using centralized file finder.

parameters.name: file_path
parameters.description: Path to the file(s) to analyze. Supports wildcards like *.txt

parameters.name: ignore_comments
parameters.description: Whether to ignore code comments when analyzing text. Can use -i or --ignore-comments flag.

parameters.name: min_word_length
parameters.description: Minimum length of words to consider for language detection

parameters.name: languages
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of languages to detect (default: all available)

parameters.name: recursive
parameters.description: Whether to search recursively in directories. Can use -r, -R, or --recursive flag.

parameters.name: show_files_match
parameters.description: Whether to show the list of files found. Can use --show_files_match flag.

examples.description: Analyze language distribution in a text file
Analyze a Python file, ignoring code comments using flag
Analyze all Markdown files in current directory
Analyze text files in docs directory, ignoring comments
Analyze all Python files recursively in src directory using -r flag
Analyze all Python files recursively, ignoring comments, using flags
Analyze all Python files recursively and show the list of files found

getHumanLanguageStats.class_doc:
Command to analyze a file or multiple files and determine the percentage of text in different languages,
intelligently filtering out programming code. Supports wildcards and recursive directory search.

Supports flags:
-r, -R, --recursive: Enable recursive directory search
-i, --ignore-comments: Ignore code comments when analyzing text

This version uses the centralized recursive file finder utility.
getHumanLanguageStats.execute_doc:
Execute the command to analyze language statistics in files

Args:
    file_path (str): Path to the file(s) to analyze, supporting wildcards
    ignore_comments (bool): Whether to ignore code comments when analyzing
    min_word_length (int): Minimum length for words to be considered
    languages (str): Comma-separated list of languages to detect
    recursive (bool): Whether to search recursively in directories
    show_files_match (bool): Whether to show the list of files found
    
Returns:
    dict: Command result with language statistics

getProgrammingLanguageStats.class_doc:
Command to identify programming language in one or more files and get code statistics.
Supports wildcards and recursive directory searching.

Supports flags:
-r, -R, --recursive: Enable unlimited recursive directory search
-rN, --recursiveN: Enable recursive directory search up to N levels deep

This version uses the centralized recursive file finder utility.
getProgrammingLanguageStats.execute_doc:
Analyzes one or more files to determine programming language and code statistics.
Supports wildcards and recursive directory search.

Args:
    file_path (str): Path to the file(s) to analyze. Supports wildcards like *.py
    detailed (bool, optional): Whether to include detailed statistics
    languages (str, optional): Comma-separated list of languages to detect
    recursive: Recursion level: none by default, -r/--recursive for unlimited, -rN/--recursiveN for N levels
    
Returns:
    Dictionary with analysis results for each file and aggregated statistics
